title: Harshat Mata Temple
tagline: Harshat Mata Temple — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Harshat Mata Temple (IAST: Harṣat Mātā kā Mandir) is a Hindu temple in the Abhaneri (or "Abaneri") village of Rajasthan, in north-western India.
overview.description: The Harshat Mata Temple (IAST: Harṣat Mātā kā Mandir) is a Hindu temple in the Abhaneri (or "Abaneri") village of Rajasthan, in north-western India. The temple is dedicated to a goddess named Harshat Mata, although some art historians theorize it was initially a Vaishnavite shrine. The original temple appears to have been built in the panchayatana style, which features a main shrine surrounded by four subsidiary shrines. Only parts of the main shrine now survive, and they have been ruined and modified over several centuries, with the tall shikhara tower replaced by a roof-dome.

topPlaces.name: Abhaneri
topPlaces.description: Abhaneri, also spelled Abaneri, is a village in the Dausa district of the Indian state of Rajasthan, situated on the Jaipur-Agra Highway. Modern day Abhaneri contains the ruins of an ancient city, Abhangari, as well as the popular tourist sites of the Chand Baori step well and the Harshat Mata Temple.

topPlaces.name: Chand Baori
topPlaces.description: Chand Baori is a stepwell situated in the village of Abhaneri in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It extends approximately 30 m (100 ft) into the ground, making it one of the deepest and largest stepwells in India.

topPlaces.name: Bandikui Junction
topPlaces.description: Bandikui Junction is a major railway station in Dausa district, Rajasthan. Its code is BKI. It serves Bandikui city.

topPlaces.name: Bandikui
topPlaces.description: Bandikui is a city and a municipality in Dausa district in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is 35 km from Dausa and located on State Highway 25 (SH-25).

Bandikui Junction is a major railway station in Dausa district, Rajasthan. Its code is BKI. It serves Bandikui city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Harshat Mata Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Harshat Mata Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Harshat Mata Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Harshat Mata Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Harshat Mata Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Harshat Mata Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Harshat Mata Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Harshat Mata Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Abhaneri, Chand Baori, Bandikui Junction, Bandikui.
